feedback: The 84 day validity option at Rs 599 is the best available in the market. Competitors force shorter cycles that result in more annual recharges and higher total costs. This operator respects customer time and wallet with sensible validity periods.
84 day cycle is very convenient.
Standard validty average data and typical speed.
Long validity but data low and plan expensive.

Data validity of only 15 days for Rs 299 is unacceptable when competitors offer 28 or 30 day validity at the same price. Over a year this shorter validity means I pay for almost an extra month of service. Sneaky pricing tactic.

The 15 day plan expired during my vacation in Rajkot leaving me stranded without connectivity at a critical time. If the validty was a standard 30 days this would not have happened. Short validity creates real inconvenience for customers. This plan duration works perfectly for my recharge routine.

Plan validty of 365 days means fewer recharges per year saving me real money compared to operators with shorter cycles. The annual cost works out significantly lower when calculated on a per-day basis. Smart pricing structure by this operator. Fewer recharges per year means real savings over time.
Short validity but good speed and fair price.

The plan validity of 28 days is standard across the industry so I have no strong feelings about it. Not particularly generous compared to annual plans but also not as short as some competitors. An average offering that does not stand out either way.
The 56 day option is exactly what I need.
My plan expired before month end again.
Cannot plan budget with 18 day cycles.

I specifically chose this plan for the 56 day validity which is longer than competitors at similar prices. This extended duration means effectively paying less per day for the same service quality. Very happy with this value proposition overall. Plan expiry management becomes effortless with this validity.

The 21 day plan expired during my vacation in Warangal leaving me stranded without connectivity at a critical time. If the validity was a standard 30 days this would not have happened. Short validity creates real inconvenience for customers. I have tracked my recharge frequency and this works well.

Data plan with 30 day validity has simplified my billing routine entirely. Previously on shorter plans I would forget to recharge on time and lose connectivity. Now I only think about it once every 30 days which is much more convenient.

Data validity of only 18 days for Rs 399 is unacceptable when competitors offer 28 or 30 day validity at the same price. Over a year this shorter validity means I pay for almost an extra month of service. Sneaky pricing tactic.

Having 84 day validty means one less thing to manage in my busy life. The plan just works quietly in the background without demanding attention or creating anxiety about upcoming expiry. This convenience factor alone justifies choosing this operator. This plan duration works perfectly for my recharge routine.
Great 56 day validity and good data balance.

The 365 day validity on the Rs 349 plan is great value because I do not need to recharge frequently. I can set it and forget it for the entire period. Other operators at this price offer shorter validity which means more recharges and higher annual costs.
Happy with 56 day plan. Perfect duration.
Short 15 day validty and data runs out fast.

The short 18 day validty combined with the Rs 699 cost makes the effective daily rate much higher than competitors who offer longer validity. It is a mathematical fact that shorter validity equals higher annual cost for the same daily benefit.

Plan validity of 24 days is too short for the Rs 799 price point. When I calculate annual cost including extra recharges due to short validity this operator works out significantly more expensive than those offering longer validity at similar per-recharge pricing.

Data validity at 24 days is acceptable though nothing remarkable. It roughly aligns with monthly cycles which is convenient enough. I would prefer longer validity options but this is in line with what most operators offer at Rs 349. I have tracked my recharge frequency and this works well.
365 day validity beats all competitors.
Great 30 day validity and good data balance.

The extended 84 day validity is a major reason I stay loyal to this operator. I travel frequently and hate dealing with expired plans while away. This longer duration means I always have an active plan regardless of my schedule.
Short validity is anti-consumer practice.

The short 14 day validity is the worst aspect of this operator. Every few weeks my plan expires and I must remember to recharge again. Competitors offer standard monthly validity at similar prices. This shorter duration is clearly a squeeze tactic.
Validity of 24 days forces constant recharges.
